title: neolloydia johnsonii
intro: Neolloydia johnsonii, also known as Johnson's living rock or Johnson's ghost flower, is a species of cactus in the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its distinctive white or pale yellow flowers that bloom at night. The medicinal properties of Neolloydia johnsonii are primarily attributed to its sap, which has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Neolloydia johnsonii can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
